commit.message:
feat: full-file database encryption at rest (AES-256-GCM)

The SQLite database file was stored in plaintext — anyone with file
access could open it with DB Browser and read all data.

Now the entire deadman.db file is encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM
with the existing DB_ENCRYPTION_KEY env var:
- File format: IV (12 bytes) + authTag (16 bytes) + ciphertext
- Key derived via scrypt from DB_ENCRYPTION_KEY with fixed salt
- Auto-migration: existing unencrypted DBs detected by SQLite magic
  header and encrypted transparently on next save
- Encrypted every 5 seconds (same auto-save interval as before)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CI 31 test errors: set pytest-asyncio fixture loop scope to session

Session-scoped DB engine fixtures create connections on one event loop,
but asyncio_mode="auto" creates per-test runners by default, causing
"Future attached to a different loop" and cascading asyncpg errors.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add structural pre-pass to locate frame bents before gate/pipe scan

_struct_pass: scans at 50mm step / 50mm thickness looking for slices with
  both a full-width h-band (beam chord spanning >= 60% of rack width) AND
  a tall v-band (column >= 1m height). Cross-bracing is rejected by the span
  filter; pipes are rejected because they don't span the full width.
  Nearby hits within 500mm are clustered to one representative bent position.

_has_full_width_hband / _has_tall_vband: pure-numpy structural checks, no
  dependency on _rasterize.

Main scan loop: uses struct_pass positions instead of uniform np.arange.
Falls back to full sweep if no frames found (safety net).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add mode-aware image filtering to interactive menu (#2)

Install mode shows only uninstalled images; remove mode shows only
installed images. Nothing is pre-selected by default, reducing the
risk of accidental mass-pulls or deletions. Empty categories are
hidden and an early-exit message is shown when no eligible images
exist. Non-interactive path (curl | bash) is unchanged.

Also hardens logging helpers against terminal escape injection by
switching user-controlled output from printf %b to %s.

commit.message:
perf: startup speed and task mutation optimisations

Four targeted fixes for the two user-reported performance problems
(slow initial load; slow task interactions on the Prime view).

1. Conditional integrity check (db/index.ts)
   Store last_integrity_check timestamp in app_metadata. Skip
   PRAGMA integrity_check(1) if it ran within the last 24 hours.
   Eliminates a full DB page scan on every reload after the first.

2. Debounced OneDrive persist (db/index.ts)
   Decouple run() from persistToUserFolder(). The IndexedDB write
   completes synchronously; the OneDrive JSON export is now
   fire-and-forget, debounced 1.5s. Removes ~100-300ms blocking
   from every task mutation.

3. getLatestWorkLogPerProject() (db/workLog.ts)
   New function using a SQL window function to return exactly one
   work log entry per project instead of fetching every entry.
   Replaces getAllWorkLogEntries() in the Prime loader.

4. Migration v12: idx_worklog_project_created (db/migrations.ts)
   Composite index on work_log_entries(project_id, created_at DESC)
   to support the window function query efficiently.

5. patchData on useDataLoader (hooks/useDataLoader.ts, pages/Prime.tsx)
   Expose an optimistic local updater from useDataLoader. Task
   status cycle, priority, and due-date mutations now apply an
   in-memory patch instead of re-running all 6 loader queries,
   making those interactions instant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add actual-consumption-based purchase prediction with evaporation calibration

Purchase predictor now uses ACTUAL consumption data, not theoretical recipes:
- Evaporation multiplier (1.40x Ramadan 12hr, 1.85x 24hr) calibrated from
  23 real daily settlements: actual 80.3ml/cup vs recipe 57.42ml
- Applies to milk, SMP, condensed milk (all boiled milk ingredients)
- Linear interpolation by operating hours for accuracy across periods
- Unit-based materials rounded UP (can't buy 0.3 cutlets)
- Unmatched products tracked and returned in API response
- Clear pipeline: predict sales → theoretical recipe → actual consumption
  adjustment → safety buffer → subtract stock → purchase qty

fix: WS 断连后 agent loop 继续运行并缓冲事件，重连自动回放

将 agent loop 从 WebSocket 连接解耦：
- 服务端：ActiveStream 维护事件缓冲区和可变 socket 引用，断连时 loop 不中止
- 重连时发送 "resuming" 信号后回放全部缓冲事件，后续事件继续实时推送
- 客户端：收到 "resuming" 清除部分 streaming 状态，回放事件重建完整消息

Fix CI test errors: ignore asyncpg ResourceWarning and PytestUnraisableExceptionWarning

The filterwarnings = ["error"] setting promotes ResourceWarning from
unclosed asyncpg connections during test teardown into errors, causing
31 test errors and an ExceptionGroup at pytest shutdown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(m4a): wire RunAnalysis, GetInterleavingAnalysis, GetNoveltyAnalysis RPCs (#107)

All 5 analysis RPCs are now wired through Delta Lake → experimentation-stats
→ proto conversion → gRPC response:

- RunAnalysis / GetAnalysisResult: reads metric_summaries, runs Welch t-test
  per metric per variant, SRM check with uniform fractions, CUPED when
  covariates are present
- GetInterleavingAnalysis: reads interleaving_scores (MapArray), runs sign
  test + Bradley-Terry + position analysis
- GetNoveltyAnalysis: reads daily_treatment_effects (Date32), picks metric
  with most data, runs exponential decay fitting

Delta readers: 3 new functions (read_metric_summaries, read_interleaving_scores,
read_daily_treatment_effects) with shared downcast helpers.

31 tests pass, clippy clean.

This unblocks Agent-6 (results dashboard, interleaving tab, novelty tab)
and Agent-5 (auto-conclude needs analysis results).

commit.message:
test(m5): audit trail completeness validation (Phase 4) (#106)

Add 4 integration tests validating that every experiment mutation produces
the correct audit trail entries:

- TestAuditTrail_FullLifecycleCompleteness: walks create → start → pause →
  resume → conclude → archive and verifies exactly 8 entries with correct
  actions, state transitions, actors, and details_json content
- TestAuditTrail_ConfigUpdateCompleteness: verifies create + update in DRAFT
  produces exactly 2 entries (create + config_update)
- TestAuditTrail_ConcurrentStartAtomicity: 10 goroutines race to start;
  verifies exactly 1 succeeds with exactly 3 total entries (no duplicates)
- TestAuditTrail_FailedTransitionsNoEntries: verifies invalid transitions
  (conclude on DRAFT, archive on RUNNING, etc.) create zero audit entries

Also adds fetchAuditRows helper for ordered audit entry retrieval and
auditActions helper for debugging output.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add BASE_SYSTEM_PROMPT with shared rules and 5 character definitions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(m3): add surrogate model recalibration trigger job (#105)

Implement RecalibrationJob that closes the surrogate prediction feedback
loop: after the prediction horizon elapses and actual long-term outcomes
are observed, it compares projected vs actual treatment effects, computes
R², and updates the model's calibration for more accurate future CIs.

- RecalibrationJob.Run() follows existing per-experiment job pattern
- Reuses surrogate_input.sql.tmpl with target metric + prediction horizon
- ProjectionReader/CalibrationUpdater interfaces (Pg + Mem impls)
- computeRSquared() pure function with edge case handling
- 8 unit tests + 7 R² subtests, 2 benchmarks
- All SQL logged with job_type "surrogate_recalibration_actual"

This unblocks M5's TriggerSurrogateRecalibration RPC (currently a stub).
